# @meli/logger

Wrapped Pino logger adapted for MELI Node.js applications.

## Description

`@meli/logger` is a Pino-based logger for MELI Node.js applications. Output format adapts to the environment:

- **Production**: text by default (`timestamp - level: message - [tag:value] [level:LEVEL]`), opt-in JSON
- **Development / Test**: colourised, human-readable output

[//]: # 'NordicSiteIgnore'

> [!NOTE]
>
> Colourised output in development and test environments requires `pino-pretty`. Install it as a dev dependency, it is auto-detected and requires no configuration.
>
> ```sh
> npm install --save-dev pino-pretty
> ```

[//]: # '/NordicSiteIgnore'

## Install

```sh
npm install --save @meli/logger
```

## Usage

### Quick Start

```js
import { Logger } from '@meli/logger';

const logger = new Logger('my-service');

logger.info('Server started', { port: 3000 });
logger.warn('Cache miss', { key: 'user:42' });
logger.error('Request failed', { status: 500, url: '/api/users' });
```

Output (text):

```txt
2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z - info: Server started - [name:my-service] [port:3000] [level:INFO]
2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z - warn: Cache miss - [name:my-service] [key:user:42] [level:WARN]
2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z - error: Request failed - [name:my-service] [status:500] [url:/api/users] [level:ERROR]
```

Output (JSON):

```js
const logger = new Logger('my-service', { format: 'json' });
```

```json
{"level":"INFO","time":"2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z","name":"my-service","msg":"Server started","port":3000}
{"level":"WARN","time":"2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z","name":"my-service","msg":"Cache miss","key":"user:42"}
{"level":"ERROR","time":"2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z","name":"my-service","msg":"Request failed","status":500,"url":"/api/users"}
```

### `Logger` Class

#### `Logger` Initialisation

```js
import { Logger } from '@meli/logger';

const logger = new Logger(name);
const logger = new Logger(name, options);
```

- `name:` `string` — Logger name. Appears as `[name:value]` in every log line and is used for `LOG_DEBUG` pattern matching.
- `options:` [`LoggerOptions`](#logger-initialisation-options) (optional)

##### `Logger` Initialisation Options

| Property |        Type        | Default  | Description                                                                                                           |
| -------- | :----------------: | :------: | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `level`  |     `LogLevel`     | `'info'` | Initial log level. May be overridden by [`LOG_LEVEL`](#log_level) or [`LOG_DEBUG`](#log_debug) environment variables. |
| `format` | `'text' \| 'json'` | `'text'` | Output format.                                                                                                        |

#### Log Methods

All log methods share the same signature: message first, tags second.

```js
logger.info(message);
logger.info(message, tags);
```

- `message:` `string`
- `tags:` `Record<string, boolean | number | string | null | undefined>` (optional)

Available methods, ordered from most to least verbose:

| Method         | Intended use                                    |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `logger.trace` | Fine-grained traces, internal state dumps       |
| `logger.debug` | Diagnostic information useful for debugging     |
| `logger.info`  | Normal application events                       |
| `logger.warn`  | Unexpected situations that don't stop execution |
| `logger.error` | Failures that require attention                 |
| `logger.fatal` | Unrecoverable errors, process about to exit     |

Each level represents a minimum severity threshold. A logger set to `'warn'` produces output for `warn`, `error`, and `fatal` only, everything more verbose is suppressed.

#### Level Management

```js
logger.level; // returns the current LogLevel
logger.level = level; // changes the active level at runtime
logger.isLevelEnabled(level); // returns true if the level would produce output
```

> [!NOTE]
>
> The `level` setter enforces `LOG_LEVEL` as a minimum severity. Setting a level more verbose than `LOG_LEVEL` throws in development and silently clamps in production.

### Environment Variables

Environment variables are read once at startup. To change log levels at runtime, use the [`level` setter](#level-management).

#### `LOG_LEVEL`

Sets a minimum severity for all logger instances. No logger can produce output below this severity, regardless of what was passed in `options.level` or set via the `level` setter.

Valid values: `trace`, `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`, `fatal`.

```sh
# Only warn, error, and fatal are visible. info, debug, and trace are suppressed.
LOG_LEVEL=warn node server.js
```

#### `LOG_DEBUG`

Increases verbosity for specific loggers by name. Matching loggers are set to `trace` level, all other loggers are unaffected and keep their configured level.

```sh
# 'db' logger at full verbosity. All other loggers keep their default level.
LOG_DEBUG=db node server.js

# All loggers at full verbosity
LOG_DEBUG=* node server.js

# All loggers at full verbosity except 'healthcheck', which keeps its default level
LOG_DEBUG=*,-healthcheck node server.js

# Only loggers whose name starts with 'api:'
LOG_DEBUG=api:* node server.js

# Multiple specific loggers
LOG_DEBUG=db,cache,api:users node server.js
```

Accepts a comma-separated list of logger name patterns. Supports `*` as a wildcard and `-` as an exclusion prefix.

Given the following loggers:

```js
const dbLogger = new Logger('db');
const cacheLogger = new Logger('cache');
const apiUsersLogger = new Logger('api:users');
const apiOrdersLogger = new Logger('api:orders');
```

| Pattern    | Behaviour                                                             |
| ---------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `db`       | `db` at full verbosity, others keep their default level               |
| `db,cache` | `db` and `cache` at full verbosity                                    |
| `*`        | All loggers at full verbosity                                         |
| `*,-cache` | All loggers at full verbosity except `cache`                          |
| `api:*`    | All loggers whose name starts with `api:` (`api:users`, `api:orders`) |

When a logger name matches `LOG_DEBUG`, its level is set to `trace` regardless of `LOG_LEVEL` or `options.level`. `LOG_DEBUG` always takes precedence.

```js
const dbLogger = new Logger('db'); // trace (matched)
const apiLogger = new Logger('api'); // info  (not matched, keeps default)
```

Level resolution: `LOG_DEBUG` match → `trace`. Otherwise: `LOG_LEVEL` > `options.level` > `'info'`.

> [!TIP]
>
> A naming convention like `service:module` (e.g. `api:users`, `api:orders`) makes `LOG_DEBUG=api:*` patterns more effective.

### `setupGlobalTags`

Registers a global function that is called on every log line to inject extra tags. The main use case is attaching request-scoped data (request ID, user ID, etc.) so all log lines within a request are automatically correlated.

This function uses [`@meli/server-context`](https://github.com/melisource/fury_node-core-libs/blob/master/packages/server-context/README.md) to retrieve the current request. **It should be called once at application startup**.

> [!WARNING]
>
> `setupGlobalTags` is intended for **application-level use only**. Libraries must not call it. It modifies global state that affects the entire application, and calling it more than once overwrites the previous mapper.

#### `setupGlobalTags` Syntax

```ts
import { setupGlobalTags } from '@meli/logger';

setupGlobalTags(mapper);
```

#### `setupGlobalTags` Parameters

- `mapper:` `(req?: Request) => Record<string, unknown>` — A function that receives the current request and returns tags to merge into every log line.

> [!IMPORTANT]
>
> `req` is `undefined` when `@meli/server-context` is not set up yet or has no active request in scope. **`req` must be validated before accessing its properties.**

### Output Formats

#### Text (default)

```txt
2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z - info: message - [name:my-service] [tag:value] [level:INFO]
```

#### JSON

Enable via `options.format`:

```js
const logger = new Logger('my-service', { format: 'json' });
```

```json
{
  "level": "INFO",
  "time": "2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z",
  "name": "my-service",
  "msg": "message",
  "tag": "value"
}
```

## Examples

### Fixed global tags

Tags that are always present regardless of context.

```ts
import { Logger, setupGlobalTags } from '@meli/logger';

setupGlobalTags(() => ({
  version: '1.0.0',
  region: 'us-east',
}));

const logger = new Logger('payments');

logger.info('Worker started');
```

Output:

```txt
2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z - info: Worker started - [name:payments] [version:1.0.0] [region:us-east] [level:INFO]
```

### Request-scoped global tags

Tags that are only available inside an active request, useful for correlating logs across a single request.

```ts
import { Logger, setupGlobalTags } from '@meli/logger';

setupGlobalTags((req) => {
  if (!req) {
    return {};
  }

  return {
    requestId: req.header('x-request-id'),
    userId: req.user?.id ?? 'unauthenticated',
  };
});

const logger = new Logger('payments');

logger.info('Payment processed', { orderId: 'abc-123' });
```

Output (inside a request):

```txt
2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z - info: Payment processed - [name:payments] [requestId:abc-123] [userId:42] [orderId:abc-123] [level:INFO]
```

Output (outside a request):

```txt
2024-01-15T12:00:00.000Z - info: Payment processed - [name:payments] [orderId:abc-123] [level:INFO]
```

### Log Levels in Practice

Each level suits a different kind of message:

```js
const logger = new Logger('checkout');

// Fine-grained tracing, only visible with trace level
logger.trace('Cart items iterated', { count: items.length, items });

// Diagnostic info, helpful when investigating a reported issue
logger.debug('Payment gateway response', {
  status: response.status,
  body: response.data,
});

// Normal operation events, always visible at the default level
logger.info('Order placed', { orderId: 'abc-123', total: 99.99 });

// Something unexpected happened, but execution continues
logger.warn('Retry attempt', { attempt: 2, maxRetries: 3, error: err.message });

// A failure that needs attention
logger.error('Order creation failed', {
  orderId: 'abc-123',
  error: err.message,
});

// Unrecoverable error, process should exit after this
logger.fatal('Database connection lost', { host: 'db.internal' });
```

By default, only `info` and above are visible. `LOG_LEVEL=debug` also shows `debug` lines. `LOG_DEBUG` provides surgical control per logger, see [`LOG_DEBUG`](#log_debug).
